title: Boat Rental in Aruba: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Boat hire in Aruba offers a range of experiences from shared tours at around $75 per person to private yacht charters exceeding $2,000 per day, with most trips departing from Oranjestad or Palm Beach. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators across the island.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Aruba?

Rental costs in Aruba range from $75 for a shared tour to over $3,000 for a crewed luxury yacht per day. Prices vary by boat type, duration and season. For a full breakdown of available vessels you can book a boat online through our platform to compare directly with operators.

  • Shared snorkel tour (half-day): $75, $120 per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day, no licence): $350, $600
  • Catamaran full-day charter (up to 12 guests): $1,200, $2,000
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full-day, up to 10 guests): $2,500, $3,500

Which boat to rent in Aruba?

The best boat for you depends on your group size, budget and whether you want to captain it yourself or hire a skipper. Here are popular choices:

  1. Azimut 58, ideal for a luxury day cruise around Palm Island; sleeps up to 10 guests; from $2,800 per day. Browse the Azimut 58 for hire to see its full specifications and availability.
  2. Axopar 28, a versatile runabout for island hopping to Mangel Halto; seats 8, 10; from $600 half-day.
  3. Lagoon 42 catamaran, spacious for a full-day snorkel trip to the Renaissance private island; accommodates 12; from $1,500 full-day.
  4. Sunseeker Predator 68, high‑performance motor yacht for sunset cruises off Eagle Beach; up to 10 guests; from $3,000 full-day.

Where to set off in Aruba

Most boat rentals depart from Oranjestad, with additional options from Palm Beach and Noord. Key departure points include the following:

  • Renaissance Marina in Oranjestad, a central hub for charter vessels
  • Bucuti & Tara Beach Resort jetty on Eagle Beach
  • Palm Beach piers near the Ritz-Carlton and Marriott resorts
  • Mangel Halto, a quiet launch point for kayaks and small motorboats
  • Savaneta, a traditional fishing village with local boat hire
  • Spanish Lagoon, a sheltered inlet ideal for paddleboards and small dinghies

Season and licence tips for Aruba

The peak season runs from December to April, with calm seas and steady trade winds, while the low season faces higher rainfall and occasional rougher seas. If you plan to captain a boat without a skipper, note that Aruba generally requires a valid boating licence from your home country, the operator will confirm the exact requirements at booking.

To avoid disappointment, book at least four weeks ahead if visiting between January and March, when charter demand is highest and the windiest conditions often occur off Malmok beach.

At a glance: Aruba boat rental

  • Price range: $75 (shared tour) to $3,500 (luxury yacht)
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical half-day trip: 4 hours
  • Maximum guests on private charters: 10, 12
  • Currency: all prices in US dollars

Before you book, it is useful to understand how boat rental works on our marketplace, from searching to confirming with the owner.

Questions about boat rental in Aruba

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Aruba?

You need a valid boating licence from your country of residence to operate a motorboat without a skipper; some operators accept an International Certificate of Competence.

What is the best time of year for boating in Aruba?

The dry season from January to April offers the most stable weather, with average temperatures of 28 °C and steady easterly trade winds.

Can I rent a boat for a sunset cruise in Aruba?

Many charter companies offer two-hour sunset cruises along the west coast, often including drinks and snacks, with prices around $90 per person.

Are there all-inclusive boat trips available?

Most crewed catamarans and yachts include open bar, lunch and snorkel equipment in their daily rate, with prices starting at $150 per person for a half-day trip.

Aruba offers year-round boating with clear waters and reliable winds. To secure your chosen date, especially in high season from December to April, we recommend booking at least three weeks in advance. Boathire24 connects renters with local operators across the island; you can also list your own vessel and check availability on our platform. If you own a boat in Aruba, consider listing it to reach thousands of visitors planning their 2026 holiday.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Aruba and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
